title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Lorraine Wade, Eileen Bondel, and Jimmy Sycamore continue their discussion regarding the murders of Gerry Wade and Ronnie DeVille. Lorraine, Gerry's only relative, expresses her determination to seek justice for her brother. Jimmy observes that they currently lack sufficient clues, noting they only possess the phrase "seven clock" without knowing its location.

Bondel proposes a theory that someone at the Chimney House poisoned Gerry by dissolving chloral in his water glass. To narrow down the suspects, she suggests reviewing the staff list to identify recent hires, specifically mentioning a new butler named John. The group considers several individuals who were present at the time, including Nancy, Helen, Socks Davenport, Gary Wade, Bill Everly, Sir Oswald, Lady Cutt, and Bettman, Sir Oswald's secretary.

The conversation turns back to the "seven clocks." Jimmy recalls that there were only seven clocks on the mantelpiece in Gerry's room rather than eight, a detail that had made him uncomfortable. He explains that the clocks were purchased collectively after Bettman suggested using alarms to wake Gerry, and Bill Everly had suggested buying a dozen. Jimmy concludes that Gerry likely discovered a secret organization similar to the Mafia, which led to his death and Ronnie's murder.

After a brief interruption by Stevens, who serves them lunch, the group resumes their discussion. Bondel reveals that George Lomax, nicknamed "Old Codfish," received a threatening letter mentioning "seven o'clock" in relation to a party he is hosting the following week. Jimmy concludes that something is planned to occur at that party.
